settings.title: Twenty Years On, Backing the Next Mechanics
settings.excerpt: Two decades after we opened the doors, we are putting our anniversary into apprentices instead of a party.
settings.body:
Torque has been turning spanners on this road for twenty years this summer. The trade has changed beyond recognition, but the shortage of young mechanics has only got worse.

So we are marking the milestone by taking on two apprentices and sponsoring tool kits for the motor course at the local college. A decent starter tool kit is a real barrier for someone leaving school, easily a few hundred pounds before they have earned a penny, and that alone puts people off the trade.

Why apprentices, not a party

Both of us who founded the garage started as apprentices ourselves, sweeping floors and watching closely. Someone gave us a chance, and this felt like the right way to pass it on. A party lasts an afternoon. An apprentice who stays in the trade lasts a career.

  • Two apprentices joining the workshop this year
  • Tool kits sponsored for the college motor course
  • A standing offer of work experience for local students

The work itself has moved on too. A modern mechanic needs to read diagnostics and understand high-voltage systems as much as they need to know an engine, so the training we give now looks very different from what we received. We would rather help build that next generation than watch the skills gap widen.

Thank you to the customers who have trusted us with their cars for twenty years. Some of you we have now seen through three or four vehicles, and that means a great deal. Here is to the apprentices keeping the ramps busy for the next twenty.
